findings.evidence: The local helpers are already generalized at lines 42-55 as `[CommSemiring R] [Semiring H] [Algebra R H]`; Mathlib defines `HopfAlgebra` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/Basic.lean:65` under `[CommSemiring R] [Semiring A]`, and `Bialgebra` at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/Basic.lean:57` under the same semiring/noncommutative assumptions.
findings.fix: State the structure and its basic API under `variable [CommSemiring R] [Semiring H] [HopfAlgebra R H]`; add a separate commutative specialization only if later algebraic-geometry code needs it.
findings.issue: `HopfIdeal` is restricted to `[CommRing R] [CommRing H]`, excluding the noncommutative/semiring Hopf algebras supported by Mathlib even though the fields only use an ideal, `Coalgebra.comul`, `Coalgebra.counit`, `HopfAlgebra.antipode`, and the already-general tensor ideals.

summary: The tensor-summand helpers are now at semiring level, but the main Hopf-ideal API is still exported under stronger commutative-ring hypotheses than the Mathlib Hopf-algebra interface requires.
